steps.title: Use the sum shortcut
steps.content: For a quadratic $ax^2+bx+c=0$, the sum of the two solutions is $-\dfrac{b}{a}$. Here $a=1$ and $b=-7$.

steps.title: Compute the sum
steps.content: $$-\dfrac{b}{a}=-\dfrac{-7}{1}=7.$$ So the sum of the solutions is 7.

steps.title: Check by factoring
steps.content: As a check, $x^2-7x-30=(x-10)(x+3)$, so the solutions are $10$ and $-3$. Their sum is $10+(-3)=7$.
